texts.content:
Hey, are you still planning to sell your used textbooks this semester?

Yeah, I listed them on the student exchange page yesterday. Three of them — intro to psych, calculus, and freshman comp.

How much are you asking for the psych book? I might be interested.

$40. It's in really good condition, no highlighting. The retail price is over $120.

That's a deal. Can I come by and pick it up this evening?

Sure, text me when you're on your way.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this conversation?
options: A. To discuss a group study session
B. To arrange the purchase of a used textbook
C. To compare prices at different bookstores
D. To ask for a recommendation about courses
questions.explanation: The exchange focuses on the sale of a used psychology textbook, from listing to price negotiation to pickup arrangement.

questions.stem: What does the seller imply about the price of $40?
options: A. It is negotiable depending on the condition
B. It is significantly lower than the original price
C. It is the standard price for used textbooks
D. It includes all three textbooks as a bundle
questions.explanation: The seller mentions the retail price is 'over $120,' implying that $40 represents a substantial saving.

texts.content:
SpeakFluently Language School

Learn a new language in just 10 weeks!

- Small group classes (max 6 students)
- Native-speaking instructors
- Flexible evening and weekend schedules
- Free trial class — no obligation

Languages offered: Spanish, French, German, Mandarin, Japanese, and Italian.

Summer enrollment is now open. Early bird discount: 15% off if you register before June 1.

Visit www.speakfluently.example.com or call 555-0147 to book your free trial.

questions.stem: What is the main selling point emphasized by this advertisement?
options: A. The lowest prices in the city
B. The convenience and flexibility of the classes
C. The opportunity to travel abroad
D. The use of digital learning tools
questions.explanation: The ad highlights small class sizes, native-speaking instructors, and flexible schedules — all pointing to quality and convenience.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the early bird discount?
options: A. It applies to all language courses equally
B. It is only available for the first 50 registrants
C. It offers a reduced rate for those who sign up early
D. It requires a minimum payment upfront
questions.explanation: The ad states '15% off if you register before June 1,' meaning early registrants receive a discount on the regular price.

texts.content:
Neighborhood Alert — Lost Cat

Hi everyone — our cat Oliver went missing on the evening of Tuesday, April 8. He's a 4-year-old orange tabby with a white chest and paws. He is microchipped and was wearing a blue collar with a bell.

He was last seen on Cedar Avenue near the park. Oliver is very friendly but can be easily startled by loud noises.

If you see him, please contact Sarah at 555-0238. No questions asked — we just want him home safely.

Please share this post!

questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of this post?
options: A. To complain about the neighborhood's pet policies
B. To ask the community for help finding a lost pet
C. To warn residents about a dangerous animal
D. To advertise a pet adoption opportunity
questions.explanation: The post describes a lost cat and asks neighbors to contact the owner if they see it, clearly seeking help in locating the missing pet.

questions.stem: The phrase 'no questions asked' in the post suggests that the owner:
options: A. does not need proof that the cat belongs to them
B. will not inquire about how or why the cat went missing
C. wants the cat returned regardless of any circumstances
D. cannot answer questions about the cat's description
questions.explanation: 'No questions asked' is a common expression indicating the owner simply wants the cat back safely without any complications or explanations.
